criteria_checked.criterion_ref: T012.1 imported token classes + fail-closed detector load (A2 MANDATORY)
criteria_checked.note: worker-result-check.py:20-68 imports SECRET_VALUE/PLACEHOLDER/find_token_material from oauth-cli-adapter.py via importlib with sys.dont_write_bytecode=True set BEFORE the load (:24); the load is exception-wrapped (:56-68) setting _DETECTOR_ERR rather than raising a module-level sys.exit, so manual-import's _load(_RC at manual-import-adapter.py:80) keeps its exit-2 fail-closed contract; main() converts _DETECTOR_ERR to a clean REJECT exit 1 (:186-187). Value-blind scan runs find_token_material(json.dumps(task),json.dumps(result)) at :279. worker-context-guard.sh:68-93 mirrors the same import + fail-closed sentinel protocol (:22-50). Adversarial suite proves all six OAuth classes (jwt/bearer/authz/access_token/gho/ya29) REJECT value-blind and PLACEHOLDER ACCEPTs.

WORKERREVLIB + M7SUITEDIR vars; worker verb arm (review-check|authorize|apply-check|fidelity); delegation exec pass-through carries append/check; run_m7_suite guarded (missing-script rc=1); worker-check + m7-suite registered in BOTH --all and named blocks; usage() heredoc + delegation help-note updated. criteria_checked.note: Dropped local OAUTH_TOKEN_PATTERNS binding is provably unused: manual-import imports OAUTH_TOKEN_PATTERNS/find_token_material from the ADAPTER (manual-import-adapter.py:82-83), only DISALLOWED/diff_paths from worker-result-check.py — no consumer of the dropped binding. find_token_material still imported (:66) + used (:279); six OAuth classes still caught (adversarial c4 rows all REJECT). Reworded comments carry 'never'. Reproduced the exact v0.2-verify grep — ZERO matches; dmc legacy v0.2-verify = PASS=23 FAIL=0 ALL PASS. Frozen dmc-v0.2-verify.sh NOT in the diff (only bin/lib/dmc-delegation.py changed in bin/lib).

git diff --name-only over pre-tool-guard/scope-guard/secret-guard/evidence-log/dmc-router/stop-verify-gate.sh, settings.json, dmc-install.sh, dmc-uninstall.sh, dmc-doctor.py, root WORKER_{TASK,RESULT,REVIEW}_SCHEMA.md, and .harness/schemas/worker-review.schema.md = EMPTY. .claude/workers/providers/** not in the modified set. The only .harness/plans/dmc-v1-runtime-upgrade.md change is the Approval-Status M7 record (orchestrator lane, authorized by the plan's allow-row).

Independently re-ran: worker-check 34/0, m7-suite 85/0, m8-suite manifest 10/0, delegation 41/0, v0.2-verify 23/0 ALL PASS, v0.3.3-verify 33/1/2 — all match the evidence file tables. The v0.3.3 lone FAIL is C9 working-tree-drift (resolves on commit). The committed-replica 802/3/3-EXACT claim and the lone rollback settings.json FAIL characterized as a squash-replica git-history artifact are fair: settings.json and rollback machinery are byte-untouched by M7.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: r2 advisory dispositions A1/A2/A3/A4 honored as-built
criteria_checked.note: A1 path-safety present with negative control (dmc-worker-review.py:197-203,438; N13,A-N4,D2); A2 exception-wrapped detector load (worker-result-check.py:56-68 + main :186-187, manual-import _load exit-2 preserved); A3 v0.2.1.1-verify added to the build individual-run list; A4 result_id disclosure in apply-authorization.schema.md:60-63.

advisories.statement: After amendment (b), worker-result-check.py binds SECRET_VALUE (:64) and PLACEHOLDER (:65) at module level but neither is referenced in-file — only find_token_material (:279) is used. Inert bindings with no external consumer; harmless, a future cleanup could drop them. Not a defect.

The v0.2-verify credential-hygiene check remains a content-substring grep over .claude/hooks/worker-* that now passes only because the two reworded comments carry the allow-word 'never'. Brittle coupling: a future reword dropping 'never' while still emitting oauth/bearer/token substrings would re-trigger the false positive. The orchestrator correctly rejected narrowing the frozen grep as out-of-scope for M7; flagged as an M9/M10 hardening candidate.

delegation append hashes the chain tip over the exact stored compact-canonical line bytes (LF excluded), so append/check are internally consistent; but the schema's 'externally-authored records can compute prev_hash' claim additionally presupposes the external author reproduces the identical canonical serialization of the prior line. Worth one disclosure line in delegation.schema.md for M9 chain consumers.
